messages.content:
Board check before I start the week.
- Sablewood Resorts Group | Director of Revenue Strategy | applied | A- | 88% | 6 days | ghost risk low | docs ready yes
- Loftwren | Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics | conversation | B+ | 74% | 11 days | ghost risk medium | docs ready no
- Wanderfare Group | Senior Manager, Revenue Science | researched | A | 91% | 3 days | ghost risk low | docs ready no
- Northline Coach Company | Manager, Commercial Network Planning | sourced | C | 52% | 19 days | ghost risk high | docs ready no
What should I do today?

Start with Wanderfare Group. It's your only A at 91% and it's still sitting in researched with no tailored docs, so write them today and apply.
Loftwren next: eleven days in conversation with medium ghost risk means the thread is cooling. Send the pricing note you owe them and get a date on the calendar.
Sablewood can wait. Six days applied, low ghost risk, docs already in, nothing you do today improves it.
Northline is a C at 52% that hasn't moved in nineteen days. Reject it and stop scrolling past it.

I have 25 minutes before a call. Board is all top of funnel and I know it.

| company | title | stage | fit | match | days | ghost | docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cinderpeak Resorts | Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution | sourced | B | 61% | 4 | low | no |
| Windward Voyages | Vice President, Commercial | sourced | B+ | 70% | 9 | low | no |
| Saltmoor Expeditions | Director of Global Operations | sourced | A- | 84% | 2 | low | no |
| Vermilion Trail Journeys | Operations Manager, Guided Travel | researched | C | 48% | 22 | medium | no |

Twenty-five minutes buys one real move: research Saltmoor Expeditions. A- and 84% is the best thing on this board and it's only two days old, so the trail is warm.
If minutes remain, skim Windward Voyages the same way. Nine days in sourced at 70% is the point where a role goes stale without anyone deciding.
Cinderpeak keeps until tomorrow; four days and a B don't compete.
Vermilion Trail has been researched 22 days at 48% with medium ghost risk. That's a no. Mark it rejected and free the slot.

One interview and a pile of dead weight. Help me not blow it.

company: Sablewood Resorts Group
title: Director of Revenue Strategy
stage: interview
fit: A
match: 93%
days in stage: 2
ghost risk: low
docs: yes

company: Loftwren
title: Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics
stage: applied
fit: B
match: 66%
days in stage: 27
ghost risk: high
docs: yes

company: Beacon Halloway Events
title: Supplier Performance Manager
stage: sourced
fit: C
match: 44%
days in stage: 14
ghost risk: medium
docs: no

Everything today goes to Sablewood. A fit, 93%, two days into interview, so prep revenue strategy cases while the conversation is still fresh.
Loftwren has been applied for 27 days at high ghost risk. Send one short note to the recruiter, then let it resolve either way; a B at 66% doesn't deserve prep time.
Beacon Halloway is a C at 44% you haven't touched in 14 days. Leave it alone this week.

Everything's old. Nothing moves.
Wanderfare Group / Senior Manager, Revenue Science / applied / A- / 86 / 31 days / high / yes
Windrose Air Group / Manager, Network Planning / applied / B / 63 / 24 days / high / yes
Loftwren / Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics / conversation / B+ / 78 / 19 days / medium / yes
Northline Coach Company / Manager, Commercial Network Planning / researched / C / 51 / 40 days / high / no

A stale board is a follow-up problem, not a sourcing problem. Three things:
Loftwren first. Nineteen days in conversation at B+ and 78%, and only medium ghost risk, which means it's the one still breathing. Email the person directly and propose two times.
Wanderfare Group is your best fit at A- and 86%, but 31 days applied with high ghost risk. One last note to the hiring manager, then treat silence as the answer.
Windrose at 63% and Northline at 51% are both high risk with nothing behind them. Reject both, then go add roles.

ok so I got turned down by three places in eight days and the board looks like a graveyard - Cinderpeak Resorts (Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution) rejected B+ 72% 5 days low docs yes, Windward Voyages (Vice President, Commercial) rejected A- 81% 3 days low docs yes, Northline Coach Company (Manager, Commercial Network Planning) rejected C 47% 8 days medium docs no, and the only live one is Saltmoor Expeditions (Director of Global Operations) researched A 90% 1 day low docs no. what do I even do

You have one live role and it's the strongest thing on the board: Saltmoor Expeditions, A at 90%, one day in researched. Build the tailored docs today. That's the whole job.
Then rebuild volume. Windward Voyages was A- at 81% and Cinderpeak B+ at 72%, both with docs ready, so reuse that material on two or three new travel roles this week instead of writing from scratch.
Nothing else needs you. Northline was a C at 47% and that rejection saved you the effort.

Big board, I keep opening it and doing nothing. Sort me out.
- Sablewood Resorts Group - Director of Revenue Strategy - interview - A - 92% - 5 days - low - yes
- Wanderfare Group - Senior Manager, Revenue Science - applied - A- - 87% - 12 days - medium - yes
- Loftwren - Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics - applied - B+ - 75% - 9 days - low - yes
- Cinderpeak Resorts - Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution - conversation - B - 64% - 6 days - low - no
- Windward Voyages - Vice President, Commercial - researched - B+ - 71% - 15 days - medium - no
- Windrose Air Group - Manager, Network Planning - sourced - C - 50% - 21 days - high - no
- Saltmoor Expeditions - Director of Global Operations - sourced - A- - 83% - 2 days - low - no
- Vermilion Trail Journeys - Operations Manager, Guided Travel - rejected - C - 45% - 4 days - low - no

Four moves, in this order.
Sablewood is at interview, A, 92%, five days in. Block two hours for prep before anything else touches your calendar.
Saltmoor Expeditions is two days old at A- and 83%. Research it now while it's warm and start the docs.
Wanderfare has been applied 12 days at 87% with ghost risk creeping to medium; a short follow-up keeps it alive.
Then clear the floor. Windrose is a C at 50%, untouched 21 days, high risk, so reject it. Windward at 15 days in researched needs a yes or no from you this week.

Short board on purpose. Which one gets my Sunday afternoon?

Loftwren | Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics | researched | A- | 85% | 4 days | low | docs no
Wanderfare Group | Senior Manager, Revenue Science | conversation | B+ | 77% | 13 days | medium | docs yes
Windrose Air Group | Manager, Network Planning | applied | B | 60% | 30 days | high | docs yes

Give the afternoon to Loftwren. A- at 85%, four days in researched, docs not written: that's the highest-value unfinished work here, and the only role where your effort changes the outcome.
Spend the last fifteen minutes on Wanderfare Group. Thirteen days in conversation at medium ghost risk is exactly when a warm thread goes cold, so ask for the next step by name.
Windrose is 30 days applied at B and 60% with high ghost risk. Don't give it a Sunday.

Weekly reset. Where do I put the effort?

| Company | Role | Stage | Fit | Match | Days | Ghost | Docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Marrow Ridge Health | Director, Ambulatory Operations | applied | A- | 86% | 7 | low | yes |
| Wexley Care Partners | Operations Manager, Home Infusion | interview | B+ | 79% | 3 | low | yes |
| Verdigris Care Network | Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs | researched | B | 68% | 5 | low | no |
| Harborlight Senior Living | Vice President of Clinical Operations | sourced | C | 49% | 12 | medium | no |

Wexley Care Partners is three days into interview with docs already done, so prep is the only item on today's list with a deadline attached. Do it first.
Verdigris Care Network comes second. Five days in researched, no tailored docs, and a B at 68% means it needs real work to compete; write the quality and regulatory angle out properly.
Marrow Ridge is seven days applied at A- and 86% with low ghost risk. Leave it. Following up this early reads as anxious.
Harborlight, a C at 49%, isn't worth a slot.

sourced sourced sourced. I know. tell me what to actually do.
Blue Heron Health Alliance ~ Chief Nursing Officer ~ sourced ~ C ~ 41% ~ 6 ~ low ~ no
Harborlight Senior Living ~ Vice President of Clinical Operations ~ sourced ~ B ~ 65% ~ 3 ~ low ~ no
Marrow Ridge Health ~ Director, Ambulatory Operations ~ sourced ~ A ~ 90% ~ 1 ~ low ~ no
Wexley Care Partners ~ Operations Manager, Home Infusion ~ researched ~ B+ ~ 73% ~ 16 ~ medium ~ no

Marrow Ridge Health, added yesterday at A and 90%, justifies a real hour. Research it and draft the docs in the same sitting so it doesn't stall at the top of the funnel like the rest.
Wexley Care Partners has been researched 16 days without docs and ghost risk has already reached medium. Write them today or admit it isn't happening.
Harborlight at B and 65% is fine to research this week, just not today.
Blue Heron is a C at 41%. It doesn't earn a slot. Cut it.

typing this on the train, sorry - Verdigris Care Network Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs, interview, A-, 88%, 4 days, low risk, docs yes. Marrow Ridge Health Director, Ambulatory Operations, applied, B+, 74%, 21 days, high, docs yes. Emberline Health Clinical Content Lead, sourced, C, 43%, 9 days, medium, docs no. panel is thursday and I'm underprepared, what gets cut

Cut everything except Verdigris. A- at 88% with a Thursday panel is the only item with a clock on it, so every free block until then goes to quality and regulatory prep.
One exception, five minutes long: Marrow Ridge is 21 days applied at high ghost risk. Send a two-line status note tonight so their answer arrives while you're focused elsewhere.
Emberline is a C at 43%, nine days untouched. Take it off the board and stop budgeting attention for it.

Three no's this month. Board's thin. Rebuild plan?
- Harborlight Senior Living - Vice President of Clinical Operations - rejected - B - 62% - 6 days - low - yes
- Blue Heron Health Alliance - Chief Nursing Officer - rejected - C - 46% - 10 days - low - no
- Wexley Care Partners - Operations Manager, Home Infusion - rejected - B+ - 77% - 2 days - low - yes
- Verdigris Care Network - Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs - applied - A- - 84% - 5 days - low - yes

Protect Verdigris first. A- at 84%, five days applied, low ghost risk. Do nothing to it except stay ready, because it's the whole board right now.
The rebuild starts at Wexley. You were rejected at B+ and 77% two days ago with docs ready, so the material is current. Ask that recruiter in one sentence what the gap was, then reuse those docs on new operations roles this week.
Don't reopen Blue Heron. A C at 46% was never the shape of this search.

Nothing has moved in three weeks and I've stopped looking at it.

role: Director, Ambulatory Operations - Marrow Ridge Health
stage: applied / fit A- / match 85% / 29 days / ghost high / docs yes

role: Operations Manager, Home Infusion - Wexley Care Partners
stage: conversation / fit B+ / match 76% / 23 days / ghost medium / docs yes

role: Clinical Informatics Analyst - Verity Ridge Health Network
stage: researched / fit B / match 67% / 34 days / ghost low / docs no

role: Associate Medical Writer - Osterby Clinical Communications
stage: sourced / fit C / match 40% / 41 days / ghost medium / docs no

Wexley is the recoverable one. Twenty-three days in conversation, but only medium ghost risk and the docs are ready. Write to your contact today and apply rather than waiting for them to restart it.
Marrow Ridge at 29 days applied and high ghost risk gets one closing note. If A- and 85% produced no reply in a month, it needs a person, not another portal check.
Verity Ridge has sat in researched 34 days with no docs. Write them or drop it, because 67% doesn't survive half-effort.
Osterby, C at 40% after 41 days: gone.

I'm overloaded and everything looks equally urgent, which I know isn't true.

| company | title | stage | fit | match | days in stage | ghost risk | tailored docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Marrow Ridge Health | Director, Ambulatory Operations | interview | A | 91% | 6 | low | yes |
| Verdigris Care Network | Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs | applied | A- | 82% | 14 | medium | yes |
| Wexley Care Partners | Operations Manager, Home Infusion | conversation | B+ | 75% | 8 | low | no |
| Harborlight Senior Living | Vice President of Clinical Operations | researched | B | 66% | 18 | medium | no |
| Larkfield Health System | Healthcare Data Analyst | sourced | C | 44% | 25 | high | no |
| Emberline Health | Clinical Content Lead | sourced | C | 39% | 30 | medium | no |

They aren't equally urgent. Marrow Ridge sits at A and 91%, six days into interview, and preparation still changes that result, so it takes the first block.
Verdigris is 14 days applied and drifting to medium ghost risk. It needs a short nudge with a name on it. Ten minutes.
Wexley is in conversation with no tailored docs. Write them now, so the moment they ask you to apply you can.
Harborlight, Larkfield and Emberline are two C's and an 18-day-old B with nothing written. Clear all three.

quick one. which of these is dead?
Verdigris Care Network | Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs | applied | B | 63% | 38 days | high | yes
Marrow Ridge Health | Director, Ambulatory Operations | conversation | A- | 87% | 5 days | low | no
Osterby Clinical Communications | Associate Medical Writer | sourced | C | 42% | 12 days | medium | no

Verdigris is dead. Thirty-eight days applied, high ghost risk, and a B at 63%. Nothing there is worth another follow-up, so settle it in your own head and move the hours.
Marrow Ridge is the live one: five days in conversation at A- and 87%, and the tailored docs still aren't written. That's today's task, before anyone asks you to apply.
Osterby, a C at 42%, isn't dead so much as never alive. Cut it while you're tidying.

Monday morning, coffee, board. Give me the order.
- Cadenza Payments - Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger - applied - A - 92% - 4 days - low - yes
- Alderport Financial - Principal Engineer, Payments Platform - researched - A- - 85% - 8 days - low - no
- Halberd Commerce - Engineering Manager, Payments - conversation - B - 61% - 12 days - medium - no
- Cordwain Financial - Analyst, Portfolio Reporting - sourced - D - 31% - 17 days - high - no

Alderport Financial takes the morning. A- at 85%, eight days in researched, and no tailored docs, so the application is the bottleneck rather than the fit.
Halberd Commerce is the judgment call. Twelve days in conversation at medium ghost risk, but a B at 61% for a management role. If you want it, reply today and say so plainly. If you don't, say that instead.
Cadenza is four days applied at A and 92% with low risk. Hands off.
Cordwain is a D at 31%. Delete it; it's been inflating how full this board feels.

Be honest with me about this board. I think I'm coasting.

| company | title | stage | fit | match | days | ghost | docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Alderport Financial | Principal Engineer, Payments Platform | applied | A | 90% | 33 | high | yes |
| Cadenza Payments | Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger | applied | A- | 88% | 26 | high | yes |
| Halberd Commerce | Engineering Manager, Payments | researched | B+ | 72% | 21 | medium | no |
| Ardent Ledger Group | Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance | sourced | C | 47% | 45 | high | no |

You're not coasting, you're waiting, and two roles have gone high risk while you did. Fix that today.
Alderport at 33 days and Cadenza at 26 both have docs in and A-range fits. Neither needs another application; each needs one human. Find an engineer on the payments side of each and ask a direct question about the team.
Halberd Commerce is the only unblocked move: B+ and 72%, docs unwritten, 21 days of nothing. Write them and apply this week.
Ardent Ledger has been sourced 45 days at C and 47%. Drop it.

onsite loop next week and I can't think about anything else. is that a mistake?

Cadenza Payments
  title: Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger
  stage: interview
  fit: A
  match: 94%
  days in stage: 3
  ghost risk: low
  docs: yes

Alderport Financial
  title: Principal Engineer, Payments Platform
  stage: applied
  fit: A-
  match: 86%
  days in stage: 16
  ghost risk: medium
  docs: yes

Halberd Commerce
  title: Engineering Manager, Payments
  stage: rejected
  fit: B
  match: 58%
  days in stage: 7
  ghost risk: low
  docs: yes

No, it isn't a mistake. A at 94% and three days into the loop means Cadenza deserves nearly all of it, and core ledger prep is the specific thing to do, not general practice.
Spend one of those days differently though. Alderport is 16 days applied at A- and 86% with risk turning medium, and a live Cadenza process is your best reason to ask about their timeline. Do that before the loop, not after.
Halberd came back a no at B and 58%. That one matched the grade. Nothing to salvage.

half hour on the train, board is basically a bookmark folder at this point: alderport financial principal engineer payments platform sourced A 89% 2 days low no docs; cadenza payments staff software engineer core ledger sourced A- 84% 5 days low no docs; halberd commerce engineering manager payments sourced B 60% 11 days medium no docs; ardent ledger group senior manager model risk governance researched C 45% 14 days medium no docs; northfell capital partners vice president risk analytics sourced D 29% 20 days high no docs. unstick me

A bookmark folder is right: the only role you've researched is a C at 45%. Convert the top two.
Alderport Financial, A at 89%, added two days ago. Read the posting properly on this train ride and move it to researched.
Cadenza Payments at A- and 84% gets the same treatment tomorrow, not today. Two real applications beat five shallow ones.
Halberd at B and 60% waits behind those.
Ardent Ledger at 45% and Northfell at D and 29% with high ghost risk are noise. Remove both now, so the board shows four things you'd actually do.

rough month.
Halberd Commerce - Engineering Manager, Payments - rejected - B - 59% - 4 days - low - yes
Ardent Ledger Group - Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance - rejected - C - 48% - 9 days - low - no
Northfell Capital Partners - Vice President, Risk Analytics - rejected - C - 44% - 15 days - low - no
Cadenza Payments - Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger - conversation - A - 91% - 6 days - low - yes
Alderport Financial - Principal Engineer, Payments Platform - sourced - A- - 83% - 3 days - low - no

Look at what got rejected: a B at 59% and two C's in risk roles. Both A-range engineering roles are still alive, which says your targeting sharpened, not that the month failed.
Cadenza is six days into conversation at A and 91% with docs ready. Push for the next step by name today.
Alderport, A- at 83% and three days old, needs research and tailored docs before it goes stale the way the others did.
That's the list. Don't add volume until Cadenza resolves.

Small board, deliberately. Am I right to sit tight on any of these?

Cadenza Payments :: Staff Software Engineer, Core Ledger :: applied :: A :: 93% :: 5 days :: low :: docs yes
Alderport Financial :: Principal Engineer, Payments Platform :: interview :: A- :: 87% :: 9 days :: low :: docs yes
Halberd Commerce :: Engineering Manager, Payments :: researched :: B+ :: 70% :: 6 days :: medium :: docs no

Yes, on one. Cadenza stays in applied and untouched: five days, low ghost risk, A at 93%, docs already submitted. There's no signal to act on yet. Follow up if it crosses two weeks.
Alderport is nine days into interview with low risk, which usually means scheduling rather than silence. Ask your contact for the next step and a rough timeline.
Halberd is where today's work goes. B+ at 70%, docs unwritten, ghost risk already medium at six days. Write them and apply this week.

Plant's on shutdown week so I finally have time. Priorities?

| Company | Title | Stage | Fit | Match | Days | Ghost | Docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hollandale Industries | Vice President of Manufacturing Operations | conversation | A- | 84% | 5 | low | no |
| Brightfield Provisions | Plant Manager | applied | B+ | 76% | 10 | low | yes |
| Brightloom Foods | Continuous Improvement Manager | researched | B | 64% | 13 | medium | no |
| Palisade Converting | Production Manager, Packaging | sourced | C | 50% | 8 | medium | no |

Shutdown week goes to Hollandale. Five days into a conversation about a vice president seat at A- and 84%, and the tailored docs don't exist yet. Have them ready before anyone asks, because that ask arrives fast.
Second, decide on Brightloom Foods. Thirteen days in researched at B and 64% with medium ghost risk is the pattern that quietly becomes a no by default. Write the docs or reject it.
Brightfield is ten days applied with everything already in. Nothing to do there.
Palisade at C and 50% doesn't earn shutdown time.

final round friday. everything else can rot, right?
Hollandale Industries > Vice President of Manufacturing Operations > interview > A > 90% > 7 > low > yes
Brightfield Provisions > Plant Manager > applied > B+ > 73% > 18 > medium > yes
Halbrook Stamping Works > Maintenance and Automation Supervisor > sourced > D > 35% > 26 > high > no

Mostly yes, not entirely. Hollandale at A and 90% with a Friday final round takes every prep hour you have. Seven days in interview means they're moving, so arrive with a plan for the first ninety days.
Before Friday, give Brightfield ten minutes. Eighteen days applied and ghost risk now medium, and a live final round elsewhere is an honest reason to ask where they stand.
Halbrook is a D at 35% you haven't touched in 26 days. Let it rot properly and remove it.

I've been adding roles instead of applying to them. Board:
- Hollandale Industries - Vice President of Manufacturing Operations - sourced - A- - 85% - 3 days - low - no
- Brightfield Provisions - Plant Manager - sourced - A - 89% - 6 days - low - no
- Palisade Converting - Production Manager, Packaging - sourced - B - 67% - 9 days - low - no
- Brightloom Foods - Continuous Improvement Manager - sourced - B+ - 71% - 12 days - medium - no
- Kestrel Precision Components - Quality Engineer, Machining - sourced - D - 30% - 15 days - medium - no
- Vandry Surgical - Supplier Quality Engineer II - sourced - D - 28% - 19 days - high - no
- Halbrook Stamping Works - Maintenance and Automation Supervisor - researched - C - 46% - 24 days - high - no

Correct diagnosis. The fix is finishing two roles, not adding an eighth.
Brightfield Provisions is the highest match on the board at A and 89%, six days old. Research it and write the Plant Manager docs today, one role all the way through.
Hollandale at A- and 85%, three days in, is tomorrow's.
Then prune hard. Kestrel and Vandry are D's at 30% and 28%, and Halbrook has been researched 24 days at high ghost risk with nothing written. Reject all three. Four roles you'll act on beat seven you won't.

Six weeks in and I've heard almost nothing. Tell me where the leak is.

Hollandale Industries
Vice President of Manufacturing Operations | applied | A- | 86% | 35 days | high | docs yes

Brightfield Provisions
Plant Manager | applied | B+ | 74% | 28 days | high | docs yes

Brightloom Foods
Continuous Improvement Manager | conversation | B | 65% | 20 days | medium | docs no

Palisade Converting
Production Manager, Packaging | researched | C | 52% | 31 days | low | docs no

The leak is that every live role is waiting on somebody else. Two applications past four weeks at high ghost risk won't turn just because they read well.
Brightloom is the exception and gets today. Still in conversation at 20 days, only medium risk, docs unwritten. Write them, then apply, because a conversation that never converts is the most expensive kind of stall.
Hollandale, your best fit at A- and 86%, deserves one direct approach to a human instead of a fifth portal check.
Palisade at C and 52% after 31 days: reject it.

two rejections back to back and honestly I'm second-guessing the whole director-to-VP jump. board right now: Hollandale Industries VP of Manufacturing Operations rejected A- 83% 3 days low docs yes; Brightloom Foods Continuous Improvement Manager rejected B 62% 11 days low docs yes; Brightfield Provisions Plant Manager applied A 88% 9 days low docs yes; Palisade Converting Production Manager, Packaging sourced B+ 70% 4 days low docs no

The jump isn't the problem. You were rejected at A- and 83% and at B and 62%, which is a small sample pointing two different directions.
Brightfield Provisions is live at A and 88%, nine days applied, low ghost risk. Leave it alone and let it work.
Today's real task is Palisade Converting: four days old, B+ at 70%, no docs. You already have tailored material from Hollandale at vice president level, so cut it down to a Production Manager scope and apply this week, and the board stops being a single role.
Don't reopen Brightloom.

Just tell me the next three actions.

| co | role | stage | fit | match | days | ghost | docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Brightfield Provisions | Plant Manager | interview | A- | 87% | 4 | low | yes |
| Hollandale Industries | Vice President of Manufacturing Operations | conversation | A | 91% | 9 | medium | no |
| Palisade Converting | Production Manager, Packaging | applied | B | 66% | 22 | high | yes |
| Brightloom Foods | Continuous Improvement Manager | researched | B+ | 72% | 7 | low | no |
| Vandry Surgical | Supplier Quality Engineer II | sourced | D | 27% | 33 | high | no |

Three actions.
Write the Hollandale docs today. Strongest role you have at A and 91%, nine days into conversation, ghost risk already medium. Turning up without tailored material is how that becomes a no.
Prep Brightfield next. Four days into interview at A- and 87% with docs done and low risk, so this is purely a preparation problem.
Then close out Palisade: 22 days applied, high ghost risk, B at 66%. One note, and if nothing returns it's finished.
Vandry, a D at 27% idle for 33 days, shouldn't be on the board at all.

Which two things actually matter today?
- Ninebark Regional Water Authority - Capital Programs Director - applied - A - 89% - 6 days - low - yes
- Rivergate Port and Airport Authority - Senior Project Manager, Aviation Capital Projects - conversation - A- - 84% - 4 days - low - no
- Kingsford County, Washington - Contracts and Procurement Manager - researched - B - 63% - 11 days - medium - no
- Foothill Community Health Alliance - Grants Manager - sourced - C - 45% - 16 days - medium - no

Two things, and neither is Ninebark. Six days applied at A and 89% with low ghost risk is a board working correctly.
First, Rivergate. Four days into a conversation about aviation capital projects at A- and 84%, with no tailored docs behind it. Build them now, while the conversation is still yours to shape.
Second, make a call on Kingsford County. Eleven days in researched, B at 63%, ghost risk already medium. Either it's worth the writing time this week or it isn't.
Foothill at C and 45% isn't.

panel interview scheduled and everything else feels like clutter.

company: Ninebark Regional Water Authority
role: Capital Programs Director
stage: applied
fit: A
match: 92%
days: 24
ghost: high
docs: yes

company: Rivergate Port and Airport Authority
role: Senior Project Manager, Aviation Capital Projects
stage: interview
fit: A-
match: 86%
days: 2
ghost: low
docs: yes

company: Havenridge County Human Services Department
role: Senior Grants and Contracts Administrator
stage: researched
fit: C
match: 48%
days: 19
ghost: low
docs: no

The clutter feeling is mostly accurate, with one exception that matters.
Rivergate is two days into interview at A- and 86%. Prep your capital delivery examples. That's the day's real work.
The exception is Ninebark, your highest match at A and 92%, applied 24 days, ghost risk high. Public agency timelines run slow, so this earns a polite status check to the hiring contact rather than a write-off. Ten minutes, tonight.
Havenridge is a C at 48% with nothing written after 19 days. Drop it.

everything here is three weeks or older and I've lost the thread
Ninebark Regional Water Authority // Capital Programs Director // applied // A- // 85% // 30 // medium // yes
Kingsford County, Washington // Contracts and Procurement Manager // applied // B // 64% // 27 // high // yes
Ridgeline Policy Collaborative // Manager, Research and Evaluation // researched // B+ // 71% // 22 // low // no
Three Rivers Regional Authority // Chief Information Officer // sourced // D // 33% // 38 // high // no

Ridgeline is the one you can still move. Twenty-two days in researched, low ghost risk, B+ at 71%, and the only missing piece is tailored docs. Write them today; low risk after three weeks means nobody has ruled you out.
Ninebark, A- at 85% and 30 days applied at medium risk, gets a short check-in naming the capital programs work you'd pick up first.
Kingsford at 27 days and high risk with a B at 64% doesn't merit a third contact.
Three Rivers is a D at 33%, untouched 38 days. Remove it.

keep meaning to sit down with this and never do. ninebark regional water authority capital programs director sourced A 90 4 days low no docs. rivergate port and airport authority senior project manager aviation capital projects sourced A- 82 7 days low no docs. fenwick housing institute senior research associate housing policy sourced B 65 12 days medium no docs. kingsford county washington contracts and procurement manager researched C 49 18 days medium no docs. foothill community health alliance grants manager sourced D 34 25 days high no docs. twenty minutes, go

Twenty minutes, one role: Ninebark. A at 90%, four days old, nothing written. Read the posting and get it to researched before the timer runs out. That's a genuine step and it's your best fit.
Rivergate at A- and 82% is tomorrow's twenty minutes. Seven days is fine; twenty wouldn't be.
Fenwick holds at B and 65%.
Use the last two minutes to delete Foothill, a D at 34% with high ghost risk and 25 days of silence, and to reject Kingsford at C and 49%. Five roles you avoid are worse than three you don't.

Got the letter today. Third one. What's left and what do I do with it?

| company | title | stage | fit | match | days | ghost | docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
